title: Kabira
artist: Pritam, Tochi Raina, Rekha Bhardwaj, Amitabh Bhattacharya

lines.translated: What kind of selfishness is yours?
You choose neither sunlight nor shade.
What kind of selfishness is yours?
What kind of selfishness is yours?
You choose neither sunlight nor shade.
What kind of selfishness is yours?

lines.note: paigambar = 'prophet' (literal). The possessor 'apna' is ambiguous in isolation (can be 'my/your/one's own'); translated conservatively as 'your'.

lines.original: Phir bhi sookha mann ke andar
lines.translated: Yet still a dryness inside the heart.

lines.note: 'Re' is a vocative particle used to call someone in song; 'Kabira' is the vocative of Kabir (the saint/poet).
Faqeera/faqeer = an ascetic or mendicant (Sufi/holy man); used here as an address.

lines.translated: Come, your shadows are calling you.
lines.note: 'parchhaaiyan' literally means 'shadows' but can also suggest memories or echoes; translation keeps the literal sense.
‘Faqeera’ (faqir/fakir) is a mendicant/ascetic; a cultural/religious address.

lines.original: Kaisa tu hai nirmohi kaisa harjaaiya
lines.translated: What kind of person are you—so unfeeling, so faithless
lines.note: ‘Nirmohi’ = indifferent/unfeeling. ‘Harjaaiya’ (harjaiyā) likely means a faithless/betraying person.

lines.translated: A cool easterly breeze watches the road
lines.note: ‘Purvaai’ = an easterly breeze (regional word). The line personifies the breeze.

lines.translated: The clay pitcher watches the road
lines.note: ‘Suraahi’ is a clay/earthen water pitcher; the line personifies the object.

lines.translated: Your lips hold salt, not sugar

lines.note: Both are Sufi honorifics/titles: 'mast' implies ecstatic/intoxicated; 'Maula' and 'Qalandar/Kalander' refer to saintly or dervish figures.

lines.original: Bujh ke yun andar hi andar
lines.transliteration: बुझ के यूँ अंदर ही अंदर

lines.note: Kabira refers to Kabir, the poet-saint; speaker addresses someone as 'Kabira'.
'Faqeer' (faqir) means a mendicant or ascetic Sufi; used here as a vocative address.

lines.translated: Come, your shadows are calling you.

lines.note: Re is a vocative/exclamation addressing the named person; Kabira is the vocative form of the name Kabir.
Faqeera (faqir/fakir) = an ascetic or mendicant; Re is a vocative/exclamation.

lines.original: Kaisa tu hai nirmohi kaisa harjaaiya...
lines.translated: How are you so heartless, how are you so lost/defeated...
lines.note: nirmohi = without attachment / heartless / detached. The word 'harjaaiya' is uncertain in meaning (see ambiguity).
